changes_summary:
Revised all three steps to address the two significant flags:

**Flag step_3 (double-subtraction):** Completely reworked Step 3. Instead of just changing the ideal from 90% to 70% in PanesContext (which wouldn't survive the double-subtraction), the plan now: (a) makes `getEffectivePaneHeights` visibility-aware by only compressing when both panes are actually visible, (b) removes the secondary `generationsOffset` subtraction in `EditorPaneTab.tsx:41-43`, and (c) uses 70% ideal only when `locks.gens` is true (not hover-open, per user answer Q3). Includes a full height-trace verification for common viewport sizes.

**Flag step_2 (hydration race):** Replaced the `[location.pathname]`-only effect with the proven pattern from `useGenerationsPaneController.ts:234-238` — the effect now depends on both `location.pathname` AND `isEditorPaneLocked`, ensuring it re-fires when the persisted lock hydrates after mount. Uses `setIsEditorPaneLocked` directly (stable useMemo ref) instead of `pane.toggleLock` (unstable identity).

**Step 1 (content shift):** Added explicit test update instructions per user note — the test at line 110 asserting `paddingTop: '360px'` must change to `'0px'`, and unused mock state should be cleaned up.

**Step 4 (tests):** Expanded with specific assertions for each affected test, including which values change and why.
success_criteria: Navigating to /tools/video-editor with editor pane locked → pane auto-unlocks (lock state persisted as false), including when lock hydrates after mount
Locking the editor pane on any tool page does NOT add paddingTop to the main content area — content stays in place
When both editor and generations panes are locked, editor pane renders at 70% of viewport height (on viewports large enough for tier 1)
When only editor pane is locked (generations not visible), editor pane renders at 90% of viewport height
No double-subtraction: EditorPaneTab uses effectiveEditorPaneHeight from context directly without subtracting generationsOffset
All tests in PanesContext.test.tsx, LayoutMainContent.test.tsx, usePaneLockPolicyState.test.ts, and useBottomOffset.test.ts pass
assumptions: The editor pane is always position:fixed and should overlay content, never displace it — so paddingTop for editor lock is always wrong
The 70% target for dual-pane mode was chosen to leave ~30% for the generations pane default height
Unlocking on navigate means setting the persisted lock state to false (not just hiding the pane visually)
When only one vertical pane is visible, no compression is needed — the pane gets its full ideal height

The 'effective heights remain stable across visibility toggle' test (PanesContext.test.tsx:291) encodes current behavior that is actually a side-effect of the double-subtraction bug — updating it is correct
